106.reason: [Logical Error: When pre is NULL (deleting the head node of the adjacency list), *ptr = entry->nextarc already correctly updates the head pointer. Executing *ptr = NULL immediately after incorrectly nullifies the entire adjacency list, causing loss of all subsequent edges.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  // Error: overwrites entire list if first node deleted and there are more nodes; should simply be *ptr = entry->nextarc;
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Logical error: overwrites *ptr set above, breaks link; should just free and continue
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Logical error: incorrectly sets the head pointer to NULL when deleting the first node, losing the rest of the adjacency list. The pointer was already correctly updated by *ptr = entry->nextarc;
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Logical Error: When pre is NULL (deleting the head node of the adjacency list), *ptr = entry->nextarc already correctly updates the head pointer. Executing *ptr = NULL immediately after incorrectly nullifies the entire adjacency list, causing loss of all subsequent edges.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect: when `pre == NULL`, it means we are at the head, so setting `*ptr = entry->nextarc` already removes the node. Setting `*ptr = NULL` here discards the rest of the list if there are more nodes after `entry`. This should just be `*ptr = entry->nextarc;` without the `else` clause.]

126.reason: // Error: After deletion, should also reduce G->vexnum and renumber vertices (shift adjacency list array and adjust adjvex values) as done in reference program. Missing this causes output vertex numbers to differ after deletion.

115.reason: Missing G->vexnum--; to update the vertex count after deletion, and failure to renumber vertices > removeVertex causes index mismatch in subsequent traversals.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing G->vexnum--; to update the vertex count after deletion, and failure to renumber vertices > removeVertex causes index mismatch in subsequent traversals.

71.reason: [The DFS traversal must visit adjacent vertices in ascending order, but this implementation traverses the adjacency list as-is without ensuring sorted order during traversal. Although insertSorted maintains sorted adjacency lists, the DFS here does not explicitly enforce visiting neighbors in ascending order because it relies on the list being sorted — however, after vertex deletion, adjacency lists may contain unsorted or inconsistent indices due to shifting, leading to incorrect traversal order.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The DFS traversal must visit adjacent vertices in ascending order, but this implementation traverses the adjacency list as-is without ensuring sorted order during traversal. Although insertSorted maintains sorted adjacency lists, the DFS here does not explicitly enforce visiting neighbors in ascending order because it relies on the list being sorted — however, after vertex deletion, adjacency lists may contain unsorted or inconsistent indices due to shifting, leading to incorrect traversal order.]

105.reason: [This line is logically redundant and potentially harmful: when `pre` is not NULL, updating `pre->nextarc` duplicates the effect of `*ptr = entry->nextarc`, possibly causing double-linking or corruption. Moreover, the logic for maintaining the linked list during deletion is flawed — `pre` is only updated in the `else` branch, so it may be NULL even when it shouldn't be, leading to incorrect pointer updates.]
